headline: PTPTN Loan Repayment Rate Stalls at 48% Nationwide
context: The National Higher Education Fund Corporation reported that only 48% of RM47 billion in outstanding PTPTN loans were being actively repaid in 2025, with 2.1 million borrowers in arrears.

cards.big: 2.1 million graduates owe PTPTN RM47 billion — and more than half aren't paying anything back
cards.sub: Average outstanding balance is RM22,400 per borrower while average graduate starting salary is RM2,300

cards.big: PTPTN collected only RM3.8 billion in repayments against RM5.2 billion in new disbursements in 2025
cards.sub: Fund sustainability collapses by 2030 without either higher recovery rates or reduced new lending

cards.big: Graduates from private institutions default at 62% versus 38% from public universities
cards.sub: Diploma holders earning under RM2,000 account for 45% of all non-performing PTPTN loans

cards.big: Salary deduction mechanism introduced in 2019 covers only 340,000 of 4.2 million total borrowers
cards.sub: LHDN-PTPTN data sharing gaps mean 1.8 million employed borrowers evade mandatory deductions

cards.big: PTPTN isn't a loan crisis — it's a graduate employment crisis disguised as a debt problem
Should PTPTN convert to an income-contingent system where repayment only triggers above RM3,000 salary?
